top_signals.title: Air France Airlines CEO on Ticket Prices, EasyJet
top_signals.summary: In an interview at the IATA annual meeting in Rio de Janeiro, Air France-KLM CEO Benjamin Smith said the airline may need to raise ticket prices to compensate for the spike in fuel costs sparked by the conflict in the Middle East. Smith also signaled he’d be willing to consider a possible involvement in a bid for EasyJet should he receive a call from investment firm Castlelake with a proposition. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Jeremy Hunt: Pension triple lock is an ‘anchor drag’ on economic growth
top_signals.summary: Former chancellor Jeremy Hunt has warned that the triple lock on the state pension is acting as “an anchor drag on economic growth.” In an interview with City AM, Hunt said that above-inflation rises to the state pension are being funded “by more debt” on future generations and called for every major party to pledge [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Corning shares jump 4% after company strikes deal to power Amazon AI data centers in U.S.
top_signals.summary: Amazon is the latest megacap company to announce a massive deal with Corning, which is rapidly becoming a critical player in the AI buildout.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Piero Cipollone: Europe’s money evolves so people’s freedom to pay remains
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Europe’s Next Set of IPOs Will Add to the Year’s Defining Theme
top_signals.summary: A familiar trend is set to dominate Europe’s batch of initial public offerings over the coming weeks: companies benefiting from higher defense spending by the continent’s governments.

top_signals.title: Food supply 'not at risk' after new Texas screwworm cases, USDA secretary says
top_signals.summary: The U.S. will lean on the same playbook it did starting in the late 1950s, part of which involves releasing sterile insects to suppress the pest's population.

top_signals.summary: Opinion | Energy Markets Limit the Hormuz Shock  WSJ
